So here's, here's how that sucker works every, like twice a day. It's going to be like, okay, we spent more than 30 bucks ever for this thing. And is it getting sales for less than 17 bucks? Yeah, cool. Let's scale the budget by 10%. It'll do that twice a day. And the way we have it set up is it'll do it once at midnight and once at noon. And that's fine. You know, like that's, that's exactly how we want that thing to run. And I'm going to just leave this at maximum right here. I'm not going to mess with it. And I'm going to uncheck email. So now we've got an automated scaling rule. All right. So it's going to vertically scale the stuff. And, by the way, if one of the dang things stops working, and, you know, we're gold, right? Like, okay, it'll, it'll, it'll turn off and it won't mess with it. So if this thing scales, for example, and it starts getting great, but then it stops converting or whatever, and we start paying more than $20 per sale, that other rule we created, it'll turn off the ad set. Now what will eventually happen is all of your ad sets will get turned off over time, right? And that's typically what will happen. Or they'll like go up, up, up, up, up, up, and then they'll start like sucking, and then they'll go down, down, down, down, down, down, down, down, you know, and you're like, what the heck is going on? And that's what happened with that one that I showed you earlier, that campaign that's been running since July. So I use this type of rules. I use these, but I do it through Magix. I'll show you how to do that in our next video. And I do it based on ROAS, right? So we can, by the way, if you want to, we're set here on this one. If you're successfully tracking the amount of sales you're getting, and that's coming through. So again, if you're using CF Power Scripts, or if you're using like that WooCommerce slash WooFunnels, Native Integration, and you're actually able to track accurately how much money is being generated as a result of your ads, you can even do this by return on ad spend. It's really simple. So we would create a new rule, right? And I'm not going to create this one because I'm not tracking it in this, with this particular setup. But we can say, okay, website purchase ROAS, right, is greater than 1.2. Let's say, all right, and if that happens, then we want to increase our budget by, let's see, 10%, twice a day. And we don't give a damn what the, you know, hey, as long as it's going, Ka-Bam-Oh. And you can do the same thing the other way around. You know, hey, if the budget, if the return on ad spend dips below 1.0, or whatever you want to do, like turn off the ad set, or if it dips, you know, like, let's say you want to scale it if it's 1.5. But if it's only around 1.2, you want to start lowering the budget. You can do all that through this very automation here. Now the catch, if you're doing it with ROAS is you need to be using something that is successfully passing your purchase values back to Facebook, like some of the solutions I mentioned earlier, right? So as long as you're doing that, man, you're gold, right? Like this is the secret weapon. I don't know why people don't talk about it that much, probably because they just don't dork out on this kind of thing very much. But this is the killer. It will eventually turn everything off, you know? So like, be aware of that. And then you'll just rebuild starting with seed campaigns. This is the same old process, but super cool, all right? So that's how you do it. If you're not using Magix and HiROAS, in the next video, I'll show you how to do it if you're using Magix and HiROAS, all right? Cool. Let's do it.
